task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early

Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===

TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS

Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===

TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS

Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===

Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with meaningful execution and commercial risks emerging. Core architecture and API gateway work are complete, and the team is progressing on data ingestion, dashboard delivery, and integration testing. However, several items are now on the critical path for the July 25 Meridian product demo and the Q3 launch-readiness plan: Meridian source data quality, large-file ingestion stability, GeoSync licensing, UAT environment access, dashboard performance, and budget overrun management.

Leadership should treat the next two weeks as a critical stabilization window. Failure to resolve the active blockers before the July 25 demo may affect client confidence and could increase the likelihood of UAT slippage, which in turn may push launch into Q4 and trigger a contractual penalty review under Section 7.1.

CURRENT PROGRESS
Overall status:
- Current project status: ON TRACK, with minor-to-moderate active risks.
- Next major milestone: Integration Test Sign-off, targeted for 2026-07-25.
- Meridian product demo: Confirmed for 2026-07-25.
- Target launch date: 2026-10-01.

Milestone and delivery progress:
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Complete.
- System architecture design: Complete.
- API gateway implementation: Complete and delivered.
- Development environment setup: Complete.
- Data schema finalization: Complete.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; sign-off targeted for 2026-07-25.

Q2 deliverables status:
- Completed: requirements, architecture, API gateway, development environment, and data schema.
- In progress: data ingestion module and dashboard v1.
- Overdue/rescheduled:
  - Integration test suite setup: originally due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
  - UAT environment provisioning: originally due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access.
  - Performance benchmarking report: originally due 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor.

Testing status:
- Sprint 14 QA execution: 10 of 15 test cases executed.
- Authentication flows: 4/4 passing.
- Batch ingestion: 5/7 passing; 2 high-severity failures remain open.
- Dashboard testing: 1/4 complete; chart rendering and 100k-record load-time benchmark remain incomplete or not run.
- Open high-severity defects:
  - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data. Blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15.
  - HEL-D-015: Large-file ingestion over approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; root cause under investigation.

Budget status:
- Q2 budget: $302,000.
- Q2 actuals: $322,000.
- Q2 variance: +$20,000.
- Primary overrun drivers: unplanned engineering sprint, GeoSync scoping work, and early load-testing infrastructure.
- Projected additional Q3 overruns:
  - GeoSync license if not renewed before expiry: $12,000.
  - UAT extension by one week: $15,000.
  - Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000.
- Commercial note: per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
- CFO review scheduled for 2026-07-18; budget reconciliation plan due 2026-07-20.

KEY RISKS
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Risk level: High likelihood / High impact.
- Current status: Active. Date format inconsistency is confirmed in Sprint 14 and is causing a high-severity QA failure.
- Impact: Could delay completion of data ingestion, impair the live-data demo, and affect UAT readiness.
- Current mitigation: Meridian committed to providing a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Owner: Amir Hassan.

2. GeoSync license renewal before demo
- Risk level: Medium likelihood / High impact.
- Current status: Active; legal review in progress with target resolution 2026-07-14.
- Impact: Could affect mapping functionality required for the July 25 demo and may create a $12,000 Q3 overrun.
- Owner listed in risk register: Raj Patel; meeting action assigned to Derek Okafor. Ownership should be clarified.

3. Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Risk level: Medium likelihood / High impact.
- Current status: Active. Benchmark task HEL-247 has not yet started; dashboard 100k-record load-time test is not yet run.
- Impact: Q3 roadmap target is dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records. Failure to validate before the demo could undermine client confidence.
- Owner: Derek Okafor.

4. Large-file ingestion stability
- Risk level: High operational concern.
- Current status: Active high-severity defect HEL-D-015. Pipeline crashes around the 210k-record mark.
- Impact: Q3 performance target is 500k records/min sustained ingestion. Current failure creates risk for both demo credibility and performance hardening commitments.
- Owner: Amir Hassan.

5. UAT timeline slippage
- Risk level: High likelihood / Medium impact.
- Current status: Active. A one-week extension has already been consumed; UAT is now planned for Aug 17-28.
- Impact: More than two weeks of UAT slippage may push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Owner: Sarah Chen.

6. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Risk level: Medium likelihood / Medium impact.
- Current status: Active. Meridian IT infrastructure diagram is due from Priya Kapoor on 2026-07-12; UAT access is expected by 2026-07-19.
- Impact: Continued delay could block UAT provisioning and integration environment setup.
- Owner: Nadia Petrov.

7. Key person dependency
- Risk level: Low likelihood / High impact.
- Current status: Monitored. No backup technical lead identified for Derek Okafor.
- Impact: Concentration risk is elevated because Derek owns or is involved in demo readiness, benchmarking, GeoSync action follow-up, and technical leadership.
- Owner: Sarah Chen.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July commitments:
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18; owner Sarah Chen.
- Complete integration test suite setup by 2026-07-20.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Achieve integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.
- Deliver Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25, including:
  - Live data ingestion using Meridian sample data.
  - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
  - Dashboard visualization with real data per contract Section 9.3.

August commitments:
- Deploy and support Meridian UAT environment.
- Conduct Meridian UAT from 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Address UAT feedback within the planned two-sprint budget.
- Continue performance hardening.

August-September commitments:
- Meet dashboard load-time target of under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Validate ingestion throughput target of 500k records/min sustained.
- Select and conduct external security penetration testing; vendor remains TBD.

September commitments:
- Complete production environment setup.
- Finalize runbook documentation.
- Finalize 30-day post-launch hypercare model.
- Conduct final client training sessions.

Launch commitment:
- Target production launch date: 2026-10-01.

Potential Q3 feature additions requiring change control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor, deferred from Q2.

ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
1. Confirm executive escalation path with Meridian for source data and environment access
- Meridian sample data is due 2026-07-15 and UAT access is expected by 2026-07-19.
- Leadership should be prepared to escalate quickly if Meridian misses either commitment, because both are tied to demo and UAT readiness.

2. Resolve GeoSync ownership and licensing path
- Legal review target is 2026-07-14, but ownership is inconsistent across documents: Raj Patel is listed as mitigation owner, while Derek Okafor owns the meeting action.
- Leadership should assign one accountable owner and confirm whether additional legal or procurement support is needed.

3. Require a demo-readiness checkpoint before July 25
- The demo must use live data, not mock data.
- Recommended checkpoint date: 2026-07-18 or earlier, aligned with the data ingestion target and demo agenda due date.
- Minimum readiness criteria should include: sample data received, date normalization defect path confirmed, GeoSync resolved or workaround approved, dashboard basic visualizations working, and ingestion stability plan agreed.

4. Intervene on performance benchmarking
- Performance benchmarking is overdue and dashboard load testing has not started.
- Leadership should request a concrete benchmark plan and daily status until at least the 100k-record dashboard benchmark and large-file ingestion failure are characterized.

5. Review budget exposure and approve reconciliation approach
- Q2 is $20,000 over budget, with estimated H2 projected overrun of $47,000.
- Internal overruns cannot be passed to Meridian.
- Leadership and Finance should align before the CFO review on 2026-07-18 and ensure the reconciliation plan is ready by 2026-07-20.

6. Protect the October 1 launch date
- UAT already absorbed a one-week extension. Additional UAT slippage greater than two weeks may push launch into Q4 and trigger penalty review.
- Leadership should ask the team to produce a launch-protection plan covering descoping options, contingency staffing, defect triage thresholds, and client decision points.

7. Address key-person dependency on Derek Okafor
- Derek is associated with technical leadership, benchmarking, demo readiness, and GeoSync follow-up.
- Leadership should appoint a deputy technical lead or backup owner immediately to reduce delivery risk.

RECOMMENDED NEXT ACTIONS
By 2026-07-14:
- Close GeoSync licensing decision or approve fallback plan.
- Confirm single accountable owner for GeoSync.
- Validate Meridian IT infrastructure diagram receipt or escalate.

By 2026-07-15:
- Confirm receipt and usability of clean Meridian sample dataset.
- Re-test MM/DD/YYYY date normalization defect path or confirm remediation estimate.

By 2026-07-18:
- Complete data ingestion module target or identify residual scope.
- Send demo agenda to Meridian.
- Conduct internal demo-readiness review.
- Align with Finance on budget reconciliation ahead of CFO review.

By 2026-07-20:
- Complete integration test suite setup.
- Submit budget reconciliation plan to CFO.
- Confirm UAT access status and environment provisioning plan.

By 2026-07-25:
- Complete integration test sign-off or document exception-based acceptance.
- Deliver live-data Meridian demo.
- Confirm post-demo action plan and UAT readiness path.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios remains recoverable and broadly on track, but the current risk profile is concentrated around demo readiness, data quality, performance, UAT access, and budget control. Leadership attention during the next two weeks can materially reduce the chance of client-facing disruption and protect the October 1 launch target.

content.text: Done. The comprehensive project health briefing has been saved as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
